selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 6.4% and 20-day momentum is 11.7%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 7.5% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.4%, downside volatility near 0.9%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.0%.
Volume confirmation is -0.7% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 3.3% versus SPY over 5 days and 1.6% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is neutral with score 0.55, confidence 0.35, and 0.1 weighted articles across 1 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow neutral at 0.59, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 1 sources, 0.1 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.59, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.63.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.01).
Overall model score is 0.127 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Visa Is Having a Rare Down Year. Is the Cash Flow Machine Finally a Bargain?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Directly about Visa's performance and valuation. Such analysis can affect investor sentiment and short-term flows (esp. if widely read), so it has modest potential to move the stock over the next 1–10 trading days. It is not a corporate press release or an earnings/catalyst event, so impact is sentiment-driven rather than event-driven.
